feedback: The output stays on topic with the Abbey Road crossing and an eye-level reference, and it uses timestamped shots with camera-angle cues. However, it diverges from the gold standard’s two-shot structure and richer static framing, and it offers limited visual detail. To better align, adopt the gold standard’s two-timestamp format, emphasize static eye-level framing in the first shot, and add explicit background cues (e.g., cars stopped, perspective lines).

fixes: Align to the gold standard’s two-shot pacing: [00:00-00:04] eye-level static shot; [00:04-00:08] the subject stepping onto the stripes toward the center.
Incorporate concrete visual cues: strong perspective lines, cars stopped in the background, and the white stripes alignment.
